name: skill_transfer_map
description: Cross-sector and within-sector skill transfer map for the Get A Job platform. Computed from role-skill mappings using weighted skill overlap (core: 0.6, secondary: 0.3, differentiator: 0.1). Direction-aware: A→B and B→A have different scores. Includes curated career logic for known real-world transitions that pure skill overlap might undervalue.

scoring.description: For each source→target pair, compute what percentage of the target role's core/secondary/differentiator skills the source role already provides. Apply same weights as fit_scoring_logic (core: 0.6, secondary: 0.3, differentiator: 0.1).

transfer_types.natural: Score >= 0.70 (or curated boost). High skill overlap, common career move. Minimal gap-filling needed.
transfer_types.stretch: Score 0.50-0.69 (or curated boost). Moderate overlap, requires intentional skill-building. Achievable with focused effort.
transfer_types.pivot: Score 0.35-0.49. Low overlap, significant reskilling required. Major career change.

adjustments: Cross-layer transfers (e.g., Analyzer→Influencer) are downgraded from natural to stretch unless score >= 0.80
Seniority jumps of 2+ levels downgrade transfer type by one step
Minimum 2 shared skills required for inclusion
Curated career transfers can boost transfer type based on known real-world transition patterns

gap_difficulty.none: 0 missing core skills for the target role

curated_transfers.description: Known real-world career transitions that are common in the Israeli tech market, validated against career path data and hiring patterns. These can boost transfer type classification above what pure skill overlap would suggest.

skill_equivalencies.description: Semantic skill equivalencies that bridge sector-specific skill IDs representing the same underlying capability. For example, 'executive_presentation' (Consulting) and 'bizops_executive_communication' (RevOps/BizOps) represent the same skill. Source role skills are expanded using these equivalencies before computing coverage of target role requirements.

Analyzer.description: Extracts insights, models decisions, evaluates risk

Operator.description: Runs processes, manages people, keeps the organization functioning

Influencer.description: Drives revenue, shapes perception, builds market presence

Enabler.description: Helps customers succeed, bridges product and user

Protector.description: Secures systems, manages infrastructure, ensures compliance

transfers.curated_note: Common exit from Big 4 risk/audit consulting into corporate FP&A, especially with CPA background.
SDR→AE is the standard sales career progression.
Marketing coordinators with communication and organizational skills move into CX.
CSM→PM is a known path when strong product-adjacent signals exist (customer insight, cross-functional work, ownership).
Implementation experience gives strong technical + customer-facing foundation for SE roles.
AEs with relationship management skills move into CSM roles, especially in mid-market.
Common exit path from consulting to product, especially with analytical and stakeholder management background.
HR generalists with organizational and process skills can move into general operations.
